whyWorks.title: Why Reviewsvisor works?
whyWorks.lightningAnalysisDesc: Reviewsvisor transforms your reviews into insights in seconds, allowing you to make quick and reliable decisions. Thanks to a clear and visual synthesis, you identify key trends and action priorities that matter for your establishment. The analysis also highlights your strengths to consolidate what works.
whyWorks.immediatelyActionableDesc: Reviewsvisor doesn't just identify problems… it proposes concrete and prioritized solutions. Each recommendation is measurable, linked to customer satisfaction, to help you act effectively and track your progress over time.
whyWorks.ultraPreciseAnalysisDesc: AI detects emotions, recurring problems and improvement opportunities with exceptional accuracy. It measures the tone of reviews and highlights weak signals to reveal the real customer loyalty levers.
whyWorks.measurableGrowthDesc: Reviewsvisor helps you increase your online rating, attract more customers and improve your revenue.
whyWorks.growthStat3: gain +0.5 to +1 point rating in a few weeks.

mainIssue.serviceHigh: Waiting time is mentioned very often negatively ({{percentage}}% of reviews). This is the first point that stands out in customer feedback.
mainIssue.qualityHigh: Food quality comes up often in negative reviews ({{percentage}}% of mentions). This is a point that affects how customers perceive your establishment.
mainIssue.priceHigh: Prices are often perceived as high ({{percentage}}% of reviews). This is a trend worth monitoring in customer feedback.
mainIssue.otherHigh: {{theme}} comes up frequently in reviews ({{percentage}}% of mentions). This is a recurring point to analyze.
mainIssue.serviceMedium: Waiting comes up regularly in reviews ({{percentage}}% of mentions). This is a trend worth watching.
mainIssue.otherMedium: {{theme}} is mentioned in {{percentage}}% of reviews. This is a point to consider in your reflection.

mainStrength.ambianceHigh: Ambience is a recurring strength ({{percentage}}% of positive reviews). This is an asset to keep according to your customers.
mainStrength.qualityHigh: The quality of your food comes up often in positive reviews ({{percentage}}% of mentions). This is a strength worth highlighting.
mainStrength.welcomeHigh: Welcome and reception are often mentioned positively ({{percentage}}% of reviews). This is an asset according to your customers.
mainStrength.otherHigh: {{theme}} appears in {{percentage}}% of positive reviews. This is a strength to keep in mind.

overall.lowRating: Your average rating is {{rating}}/5, below the sector average. This is a trend to analyze in customer feedback.
overall.goodRating: Your average rating is {{rating}}/5. The points that come up often in reviews could be areas for improvement.
overall.excellentRating: Your average rating is excellent ({{rating}}/5). The strengths mentioned in reviews are assets to preserve.

ratio.negativeHigh: {{percentage}}% negative reviews in your feedback. This is a trend to analyze, as the main issue often comes back.
ratio.positiveHigh: {{percentage}}% positive reviews in your feedback. The strengths mentioned are assets to highlight.

response.low: You respond to less than {{percentage}}% of reviews. This is a trend to analyze in how you manage customer feedback.
response.high: You respond to {{percentage}}% of reviews. This is a good practice in managing customer feedback.

consultantAdvice.secondaryIssue: In addition to the main issue, {{theme}} also comes up often ({{percentage}}% of reviews). This is a secondary point to analyze.
consultantAdvice.secondaryStrength: {{theme}} comes up often in positive reviews ({{percentage}}%). This is a strength to keep in mind.

retention.recentPositive: You have {{count}} recent positive reviews. This is a positive trend in customer feedback.

continuousImprovement.thirdIssue: {{theme}} comes up again ({{percentage}}% of reviews). This is a secondary point to watch.

differentiation.ambiance: Ambience is a recurring strength according to your customers. This is an asset to keep.
differentiation.quality: Your food is often appreciated in reviews. This is a strength to highlight.

proactive.recentNegative: You have {{count}} recent negative reviews. This is a trend to monitor in customer feedback.

globalExperience.veryPositive: {{percentage}}% of your customers give 5 stars. This is a very positive trend in feedback.

help.subtitle: Find quick answers to your questions and discover how to use
help.frequentQuestionsHint: Click any question below to ask it instantly, or enter anything related to your reviews, rating, or customer issues.
help.teamHere: Our team is here to help you use
help.faq1Question: How do I import my reviews?
help.faq1Answer:
You can import your reviews in several ways:

1. **Manual import**: Use the import tool to paste reviews or upload a CSV/JSON file.

2. **Google import**: Connect your Google Business account to automatically sync your Google reviews.

3. **CSV import**: Upload a CSV file with your reviews (columns: review text, source, date).
help.faq2Question: How does the AI analysis work?
help.faq2Answer: Reviewsvisor uses advanced artificial intelligence to analyze your customer reviews. The tool reads each comment, identifies tone and emotion, extracts important keywords, and automatically detects strengths and weaknesses. You then get a clear summary with priority improvement areas.
help.faq3Answer: Yes! With the Pro subscription, you can manage multiple establishments from a single dashboard. Each location has its own reports, filters, and analyses, while maintaining a global view of group performance.
help.faq4Answer: Reviewsvisor uses artificial intelligence models trained on millions of real customer reviews. The average accuracy rate exceeds 90%, with continuous improvement through machine learning from new reviews analyzed.
help.faq5Answer: We accept all major credit cards (Visa, Mastercard, American Express) through our secure Stripe payment system. Payment is processed securely and your subscription is activated immediately after payment confirmation.
help.faq6Question: What should I implement to address my priority issues?
help.faq7Question: How can I get more positive reviews?
help.faq8Question: Would I increase my revenue with a better rating?
help.faq9Question: How can I quickly reduce negative reviews?
help.faq10Question: Where am I losing the most customers today?

features.title: Features of
features.subtitle: Transform your customer reviews into strategic insights with advanced AI analysis
features.seeReputationDesc1: Behind every rating, there's an emotion.
features.seeReputationDesc2: captures these nuances to offer you a human reading
features.seeReputationDesc3: of your reputation. You finally discover what your customers really feel.
features.intelligenceUnderstands: An intelligence that understands your customers
features.intelligenceDesc1: Our AI reads reviews like a person: it identifies tone, word choice, emotions, and dominant themes.
features.intelligenceDesc2: It transforms the noise of comments into a clear vision
features.clearActionDesc1: doesn't just analyze: it shows you where to act. In seconds,
features.clearActionDesc2: you identify areas to strengthen and those that already make a difference
features.visibleEvolutionDesc1: Your reputation is alive. Our tool tracks your progress, detects perception changes, and
features.visibleEvolutionDesc2: helps anticipate trends before they become critical
features.localGlobalDesc1: Whether you manage a single establishment or an entire network,
features.localGlobalDesc2: offers you a clear view of your performance at every scale. Compare your sites, identify regional differences, and
features.localGlobalDesc3: adjust your strategies where it matters most
features.reportsDesc1: Clear visuals, useful data, and accessible interpretation.
features.reportsDesc2: offers you the truth about your reputation
features.partnerNotJustTool: A partner, not just a tool
features.partnerDesc2: learns to know your establishment and guides you toward excellence
features.faq1Answer: Reviewsvisor uses advanced artificial intelligence to analyze your customer reviews. The tool reads each comment, identifies tone and emotion, extracts important keywords, and automatically detects strengths and weaknesses. You then get a clear summary with priority improvement areas.
features.faq2Question: How is Reviewsvisor different from a simple statistics tool?
features.faq2Answer: Reviewsvisor doesn't stop at numbers. The AI analyzes tone, vocabulary, and emotions in each review to reveal customers' true feelings. The tool identifies trends, strengths, and improvement areas, offering a strategic vision rather than a simple data table.
features.faq3Answer: The AI groups customer feedback by recurring themes (welcome, quality, price, service, ambiance, etc.). It measures the frequency and tone of each subject to establish a clear list of high-impact improvement priorities.
features.faq4Question: What is the accuracy of the analysis?
features.faq4Answer: Reviewsvisor uses artificial intelligence models trained on millions of real customer reviews. The average accuracy rate exceeds 90%, with continuous improvement through machine learning from new reviews analyzed.
features.faq5Question: Can AI detect fake reviews or suspicious reviews?
features.faq5Answer: Yes, partially. Reviewsvisor identifies suspicious reviews based on several signals: keyword repetition, unusual writing patterns, emotional extremes, or abnormal activity over a short period. This feature is designed to alert establishments to possible anomalies.
features.faq6Question: Can I compare my performance to that of my competitors?
features.faq6Answer: This feature is coming very soon! Reviewsvisor will soon integrate advanced competitive analysis, allowing you to compare your key indicators to those of your direct competitors. A true intelligent benchmarker to track your market position.
features.faq7Question: Can I connect multiple establishments to a single account?
features.faq7Answer: Yes. Reviewsvisor allows you to centralize the management of multiple establishments from a single dashboard. Each location has its own reports, filters, and analyses, while maintaining a global view of group performance.

overview.title: Overview
overview.kpiTooltip: KPI (Key Performance Indicator): A metric used to quickly track overall performance. Benefit: You identify the key trends of your establishment at a glance.

trendTooltip.insufficient: Insufficient data to calculate the trend. At least 5 reviews with dates are required.
trendTooltip.partial: Partial data: trend calculated over a short period by comparing the beginning and end of the review series.
trendTooltip.description: Percentage change (%) in the average rating over the last 3 months compared to the preceding 3 months.
trendTooltip.up: Upward — Improvement observed over the analyzed period.
trendTooltip.down: Downward — Decline observed over the analyzed period.

trendComparison.partial: Comparison: start vs. end of period

pareto.tooltipInfo: Pareto Analysis: A method that reveals which problems generate the majority of dissatisfaction. Benefit: You focus your efforts on the causes that will have the greatest impact.
pareto.noData: No data available to generate an analysis.
pareto.frequencyNote: <bold>Note:</bold> "Mentions" represent the <bold>frequency</bold> with which a theme appears in reviews, not the perceived severity of the problem.
pareto.strengthsFrequencyNote: <bold>Note:</bold> "Mentions" represent the <bold>frequency</bold> with which a strength appears in positive reviews.
pareto.issuesParetoInfo: <bold>Pareto Principle:</bold> Issues before the 80% threshold represent the bulk of the impact. Focus your efforts on these priorities.
pareto.strengthsParetoInfo: <bold>Pareto Principle:</bold> Strengths before the 80% threshold represent the majority of your satisfaction drivers. Highlight them to reinforce your image.
pareto.issuesParetoPrinciple: According to the Pareto principle, <bold>{{percentage}}% of negative reviews</bold> come from <bold>{{count}} main cause(s)</bold>:
pareto.issuesItem: {{rank}}. <bold>{{name}}</bold> accounts for <bold>{{percentage}}%</bold> of issues with {{count}} mention(s)
pareto.issuesRecommendation: By focusing on improving <bold>{{name}}</bold>, you could significantly reduce negative feedback.
pareto.strengthsItem: {{rank}}. <bold>{{name}}</bold> accounts for <bold>{{percentage}}%</bold> of strengths with {{count}} positive mention(s)
pareto.strengthsRecommendation: These elements are your <bold>loyalty drivers</bold>. Highlight them in your communication!

rootCause.title: Root Cause Analysis: {{problem}}
rootCause.noCauses: No specific cause could be identified from the available data.
rootCause.fieldAnalysisRecommended: An on-site analysis would be recommended.

methodNote.title: Methodological note:
methodNote.body: This analysis is based solely on themes, keywords, and verbatims extracted from customer reviews. Probability levels are indicative and should be validated through on-site observation.

ishikawa.title: AI diagnosis of causes
ishikawa.subtitle: Identify the probable causes of your detected problems
ishikawa.content: Root Cause Analysis (Ishikawa): A tool for identifying the underlying causes of a recurring problem. Benefit: You understand why a problem occurs and can address it at the source.
ishikawa.dataSrc: Data from the Pareto chart of negative reviews.
ishikawa.automatedSummaryDescription: Review analysis suggests that the "${problem}" issue may stem from multiple sources—further on-site investigation is recommended.
ishikawa.questionareSkipped: Questionnaire skipped — effort level is auto-detected
ishikawa.questionareWarning: You have not completed the questionnaire for this issue.

syntheseAndDiagnostic.title: Synthesis and Diagnostic
syntheseAndDiagnostic.themePresencePercentage: % = percentage of reviews in which the theme appears
syntheseAndDiagnostic.decisionPhraseWithStrengths: Improve "{{problem}}" while continuing to build on "{{strengths}}".

confidence.lowTooltipText: This analysis is based on a limited number of reviews. The observed trends are indicative and may evolve as more reviews are collected.
confidence.mediumTooltipText: This analysis is based on a moderate volume of reviews. The observed trends are reliable but may still evolve with more data.
confidence.highTooltipText: This analysis is based on a large volume of reviews. The observed trends are reliable and representative of the overall customer experience.
confidence.tooltipSecondary: The more reviews you receive, the more precise and reliable the analysis becomes.

tocSidebar.title: On this analysis
tocSidebar.description: Click on a section to jump to it.

lexique.title: Lexicon and Definitions
lexique.kpiDescription: A KPI (Key Performance Indicator) is a quantitative measure used to assess the extent to which a defined objective has been achieved. It serves to track performance trends over time and to support management decision-making.
lexique.paretoAnalysisDescription: Pareto analysis is a prioritization method based on the principle that a limited number of causes produce the majority of effects. It enables the identification of the few factors that contribute most to a result—for example, problems, defects, or dissatisfaction.
lexique.rootCauseAnalysisDescription: Ishikawa root cause analysis (or fishbone diagram) is a visual cause-and-effect tool that structures the potential causes of a problem. The causes are grouped into broad categories, which may vary depending on the context (for example, processes, methods, resources, or environment).
lexique.aiTrustDescription: AI confidence refers to an indicator of the degree of reliability of the results produced by a model, based on the quantity, quality, and consistency of the data used. It reflects the level of uncertainty associated with the analyses and should be interpreted as a confidence level rather than as an absolute truth.

workforce.name: Workforce
workforce.fallback: Issues related to staff behaviour or competencies

causes.disrespectful: Disrespectful or impolite behaviour by staff toward customers
causes.untrained: Lack of training or insufficient staff competencies
causes.inattentive: Staff inattentive or negligent toward customer requests

causes.waitTime: Excessive wait times due to poor flow management
causes.delays: Processing delays that are too long or not met
causes.complaints: Complaint or return process poorly managed
causes.disorganized: Service organisation needs review — unclear processes

methods.name: Methods
methods.fallback: Unsuitable working methods impacting service quality

causes.coordination: Lack of coordination between service teams
causes.inconsistent: Inconsistent service practices depending on situation
causes.noFollowUp: No follow-up or response to customer requests

tools.name: Tools & Systems
tools.fallback: Failing tools or systems impacting service

causes.crashes: Failures or malfunctions in technological tools
causes.payment: Issues with payment or billing systems
causes.performance: Insufficient performance of systems or applications
causes.outdated: Outdated tools no longer meeting operational needs

causes.cleanliness: Lack of cleanliness or hygiene in premises and equipment
causes.odor: Unpleasant odour issues affecting customer experience
causes.pests: Presence of pests or contamination — critical hygiene issue
causes.noise: Disruptive sound environment affecting customer experience
causes.temperature: Insufficient thermal comfort or ventilation conditions
causes.access: Difficult accessibility or location for customers

product.name: Product / Service
product.fallback: Product or service quality not meeting customer expectations

causes.temperature: Products served at the wrong temperature
causes.taste: Insufficient taste or sensory quality of the product
causes.wrongOrder: Incorrect order or not matching the description
causes.portions: Portions or quantities insufficient relative to the price
causes.pricing: Insufficient value for money in customers' view
causes.freshness: Freshness or cooking issues with products
causes.damaged: Damaged products or defective packaging on delivery

summary.dominant: The dominant causes of the "{{problem}}" issue are primarily related to {{main}}.
summary.dominantWithOthers: The dominant causes of the "{{problem}}" issue are primarily related to {{main}}, with contributions from {{others}}.
summary.possible: The likely causes of the "{{problem}}" issue appear to be related to {{cause}}, requiring further analysis.
summary.occasional: Occasional causes have been identified for "{{problem}}": {{cause}}.
summary.noData: The review analysis does not allow a clear cause to be determined for "{{problem}}" — an on-site investigation is recommended.
summary.globalFallback: Issue related to "{{problem}}" identified in negative reviews

smart.reviewHint: AI has generated this objective. Review and adjust before saving.

service.high_1: {{count}} customers mention excessive wait times → brief the team on service speed
service.high_2: Wait time issue appears in {{count}} reviews → observe service tonight to identify bottlenecks
service.low: {{count}} customers complain about wait times → remind the team to serve quickly

quality.high_1: Food quality issue appears in {{count}} reviews → check kitchen process tonight
quality.high_2: {{count}} customers mention cooking issues → observe food preparation in the kitchen
quality.low: {{count}} customers report a quality issue → check with the kitchen if the problem is resolved

price.default: {{count}} customers find prices high → observe customer reactions to prices and note complaints

welcome.high: {{count}} customers mention a cold welcome → discuss the importance of smiling and warm greeting with the team
welcome.low: {{count}} customers complain about the welcome → debrief the team on customer greeting

noise.default: {{count}} customers mention noise or ambience → identify sources of noise disturbance tonight

cleanliness.high: {{count}} customers report cleanliness issues → check table and restroom cleanliness tonight
cleanliness.low: {{count}} customers mention cleanliness → observe table cleaning between services

other.default: {{count}} customers mention "{{theme}}" → analyse reviews on this point and debrief the team

businessTypeOverrideModal.description: Select the correct business type to improve analysis accuracy.

prediction.negativeDesc: Your rating could drop to <rating>{{rating}}</rating> if no action is taken
prediction.neutralDesc: Your rating is likely to stay around <rating>{{rating}}</rating>
prediction.positiveDesc: Your rating is likely to stay around <rating>{{rating}}</rating> or improve slightly
